what: Fast Focal and Speckle Filters for Matrices and Arrays
title: Fast Focal and Speckle Filters for Matrices and Arrays
text: Minimal, blazing-fast moving-window filters R matrices 3-D arrays, methods terra SpatRaster gdalraster GDALRaster objects, powered Rust rayon. Speckle filters SAR intensity data: rf_lee(), rf_enhanced_lee(), rf_lee_sigma(), rf_lee_sigma_improved() (Lee et al. 2009, SNAP-style), rf_frost(), rf_kuan(), rf_gamma_map(). Smoothing: rf_mean() (boxcar), rf_gaussian() (separable), rf_median(), plus edge-preserving rf_bilateral() rf_guided() (et al. 2013, window-size-independent cost). Focal statistics: rf_focal() min, max, range, sd, sum, mode. Convolution edges: rf_convolve() arbitrary kernels, rf_sobel(), rf_laplacian(). Parallel default: cores load; tune rf_set_threads(). Results bitwise identical whatever thread count. NA-aware default: missing cells excluded every window statistic (na_policy = "omit"), documented fast path (na_policy = "propagate") compiles NA handling inner loop entirely: NA window makes cell NA. Choose data missing values. Four edge policies: "shrink" (default), "reflect", "nearest", "constant". Mean, sum, sd single-pass speckle filters (Lee, enhanced Lee, Kuan, Gamma-MAP) run separable sliding-sum engine whose per-cell cost independent window size, 21 x 21 window costs 3 x 3.

title: Fast Focal and Speckle Filters for Matrices and Arrays
text: package ships s1_sthelens, real Sentinel-1 backscatter patch crater Mount St Helens, rf_plot(), small percentile-stretch image helper:  Focal statistics, smoothing convolution share engine:
code: library(rustyfilters)  op <- par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(1, 1, 2, 1)) rf_plot(s1_sthelens, main = "Sentinel-1 VV, Mount St Helens") rf_plot(rf_lee(s1_sthelens, window = 7L, looks = 1), main = "rf_lee()") par(op) op <- par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(1, 1, 2, 1)) rf_plot(rf_focal(volcano, window = 5L, stat = "sd"), main = "focal sd") rf_plot(rf_sobel(volcano), main = "Sobel gradient") par(op)

title: Bilateral filter — rf_bilateral
text: Smooths weights product spatial Gaussian (distance centre cell, sigma_d) range Gaussian (difference centre value, sigma_r), averaging happens within regions similar value sharp transitions survive.

title: Bilateral filter — rf_bilateral
code: rf_bilateral(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'matrix' rf_bilateral(   x,   sigma_d = 1.5,   sigma_r = NULL,   window = NULL,   edge = c("shrink", "reflect", "nearest", "constant"),   edge_value = 0,   na_policy = c("omit", "propagate"),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'array' rf_bilateral(   x,   sigma_d = 1.5,   sigma_r = NULL,   window = NULL,   edge = c("shrink", "reflect", "nearest", "constant"),   edge_value = 0,   na_policy = c("omit", "propagate"),   ... )  # Default S3 method rf_bilateral(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'Rcpp_GDALRaster' rf_bilateral(x, sigma_d = 1.5, sigma_r = NULL, window = NULL, ...)  # S3 method for class 'SpatRaster' rf_bilateral(x, ...)

title: Bilateral filter — rf_bilateral
text: x numeric matrix 3-D array (filtered layer layer). Methods also provided terra SpatRaster objects (terra installed) open gdalraster GDALRaster datasets (gdalraster installed). GDALRaster methods return new GDALRaster object open update mode Float64 dataset source's geometry. Small datasets filtered memory land -memory /vsimem GTiff default; datasets whose decoded size exceeds options(rustyfilters.block_memory) (default 2 GiB) stream full-width row bands halo sized filter's window, writing GeoTIFF tempfile instead. Interior band seams exact (halo supplies true neighbouring data; edge fires real raster edges). GDALRaster methods accept three extra arguments: filename (output path, replacing tempfile//vsimem default), by_block (TRUE/FALSE force forbid streaming) block_rows (rows per band, sized memory budget default). ... Passed methods. sigma_d Single positive number: spatial standard deviation cells. sigma_r Single positive number: range standard deviation value units. Values differing centre much sigma_r effectively excluded. default NULL uses standard deviation valid cells x, serviceable starting point expect tune. window Window size: single odd integer c(rows, cols) pair. default NULL uses 2 * ceiling(2 * sigma_d) + 1. edge treat windows overhang matrix edge: "shrink" (default) truncates window cells exist; "reflect" mirrors matrix across boundary; "nearest" repeats closest edge cell; "constant" pads edge_value. edge_value Single number used pad edge = "constant". NA allowed behaves missing data na_policy = "omit". na_policy treat missing values inside window. default "omit" excludes statistics (like na.rm = TRUE); window valid cells yields NA. "propagate" fast path: per-cell NA handling compiled inner loop, NA window makes result NA. Use input missing values (spreading NA acceptable) maximum speed.

title: Enhanced Lee speckle filter — rf_enhanced_lee.Rcpp_GDALRaster
text: enhanced Lee filter (Lopes et al. 1990) splits window three regimes variation coefficient ci: homogeneous windows (ci <= cu) take window mean, heterogeneous windows blend mean centre exponential damping weight, point targets (ci >= cmax = sqrt(1 + 2/looks)) preserved untouched.

title: Gamma-MAP speckle filter — rf_gamma_map.Rcpp_GDALRaster
text: Gamma maximum posteriori filter (Lopes et al. 1990), assuming Gamma-distributed signal speckle. Homogeneous windows (ci <= cu) take window mean, point targets (ci >= cmax) preserved, MAP estimate solves = (B * m + sqrt(m^2 * B^2 + 4 * alpha * looks * m * x)) / (2 * alpha) alpha = (1 + cu^2) / (ci^2 - cu^2) B = alpha - looks - 1.

Passed methods. edge treat windows overhang matrix edge: "shrink" (default) truncates window cells exist; "reflect" mirrors matrix across boundary; "nearest" repeats closest edge cell; "constant" pads edge_value. edge_value Single number used pad edge = "constant". NA allowed behaves missing data na_policy = "omit". na_policy treat missing values inside window. default "omit" excludes statistics (like na.rm = TRUE); window valid cells yields NA. "propagate" fast path: per-cell NA handling compiled inner loop, NA window makes result NA. Use input missing values (spreading NA acceptable) maximum speed.

title: rustyfilters 0.0.0.9000
text: New s1_sthelens dataset: real Sentinel-1 RTC backscatter patch (VV, linear power, 300 x 300 10 m) crater Mount St Helens, realistic speckle-filter examples. Contains modified Copernicus Sentinel data (2024). New rf_plot() helper: correctly oriented image() plots percentile stretch Inferno palette; documentation examples now plot instead printing matrices. GDALRaster methods stream rasters larger options(rustyfilters.block_memory) (default 2 GiB) full-width row bands filter-sized halo, writing GeoTIFF tempfile (filename). Interior band seams exact; by_block block_rows control behaviour. rf_lee_sigma_improved() computes global 98th percentile exactly streaming, tiled results match whole-image run bit bit. filters gained methods open gdalraster GDALRaster datasets (gdalraster Suggests): results returned new GDALRaster object -memory /vsimem GTiff, written filename. Added convolution gradient filters (rf_convolve(), rf_sobel(), rf_laplacian()), edge-preserving smoothers (rf_bilateral(), rf_guided()) improved Lee sigma speckle filter Lee et al. (2009) (rf_lee_sigma_improved()). Results now bitwise identical across thread counts every filter: filters execute one shared parallel code path dedicated rayon pool, whatever rf_set_threads() set . Initial release: SAR speckle filters (rf_lee(), rf_enhanced_lee(), rf_lee_sigma(), rf_frost(), rf_kuan(), rf_gamma_map()), smoothing filters (rf_mean(), rf_gaussian(), rf_median()) focal statistics (rf_focal()) matrices, 3-D arrays terra SpatRaster objects, thread control via rf_set_threads().
